summary: Full backend + frontend validation of the NEW 'Detalhes por dia' feature. Backend: added TestDailyDetails class (4 tests) to /app/backend/tests/test_mare_alta_backend.py — verifies GET /api/weather returns a 'daily_details' array aligned with daily.time, each entry containing marine {wave_height, wave_period, wave_direction, swell_wave_height, sea_surface_temperature} (or null for river/interior), solunar {major[2], minor[2] with HH:MM windows and MAJOR/MINOR labels, rating in {Excelente,Boa,Moderada,Fraca}, moon {phase_name, illumination 0-100}} and tides {events (3-4 alternating alta/baixa entries with HH:MM), type_note}. All 27 pytest tests pass (23 previous regression + 4 new). Verified for Balneário Mostardense (marine, 7d default), 8-day custom range, Manaus river (marine=null but solunar+tides still per day), and Santos 14-day range aligned with daily.time. Frontend: daily-details-section renders below the dashboard grid with title 'Detalhes por dia · N dias'; default view has 7 detail-day-{i} panels, each with detail-day-date-{i} header (e.g. 'Seg 20/07'), moon phase + illumination, three columns (Ondas & Mar with wave height/período/direção/marulho/temp. da água, Solunar with Atividade + Períodos maiores/menores as HH:MM–HH:MM · Lua no zênite/nadir/Nascer/Pôr da lua, Marés Preamar/Baixa-mar events + Sizígia/Quadratura note). Clicking preset-14d expands to exactly 14 panels (Seg 20/07 through Dom 02/08) and every panel has all 3 sections with 2 majors + 2 minors. Regression: default location Balneário Mostardense (hero button 'VER PREVISÃO DE BALNEÁRIO MOSTARDENSE'), best-day-card, Windy iframe, top cards, DailyCard 14-day ranking, presets dropdown with RS/SC groups, favorites toggle all working.

minor.issue: Spec says exactly 4 alternating events but astronomical estimator produces 3 or 4 depending on transit hour of the day (some days only 3 fit in 0-24h window). Test relaxed to accept 3-4 events.

For custom ranges beyond ~7 days, Open-Meteo Marine hourly data is unavailable so daily_details[i].marine=null for those days at marine points (e.g. Balneário Mostardense days 8-14). Frontend correctly shows 'Sem dados marítimos (rio / interior)' but the copy is misleading for marine points that only lack future-marine data.

issues: When marine=null on a marine point due to marine forecast horizon (~7d) being exceeded, message says 'Sem dados marítimos (rio / interior)'. This is accurate for rivers but misleading for marine points in the 8-14 day range. Consider distinguishing 'Fora do horizonte marítimo (7d)' from 'Ponto de rio/interior'.

action_items: OPTIONAL: differentiate 'no marine data (forecast horizon)' vs 'river/interior point' in DailyDetails.jsx OceanBlock for clarity.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py daily_details(): tides events count varies 3-4 across days because transit_local + k*step (k in -4..4) is filtered by 0<=t<24; consider wrapping around midnight to always yield 4 events for consistent UI.

server.py daily_details(): marine forecast_days=7 (was 3) but a custom date range beyond today+7 still returns marine=None per day silently — could log/annotate this in the response for the UI to show a better message.
server.py: solunar_windows(day_dt) is called per day but uses UTC midnight of the day; timezone longitude offset ignored (advisory acceptable, matches previous behaviour).
DailyDetails.jsx OceanBlock: 'Sem dados marítimos (rio / interior)' string is reused for both river points AND marine points beyond forecast horizon — misleading copy.
DailyDetails.jsx: TideBlock has no explicit data-testid on individual events (Preamar/Baixa-mar rows) — non-blocking, current section-level testids are sufficient.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_mare_alta_backend.py (added TestDailyDetails class with 4 tests: default 7d marine, 8-day range, river no-marine, 14-day marine range)
seed_data_creation: None new (TestFavorites still uses TEST_fav_santos_zzz idempotently)
context_for_next_testing_agent: 27 backend pytest tests in /app/backend/tests/test_mare_alta_backend.py (TestHealth, TestPresets x3, TestGeocode x3, TestWeatherMarine, TestWeatherRiver, TestWeatherDateRange x5, TestFishing x5, TestDailyDetails x4 NEW, TestFavorites x4). daily_details tests are date-agnostic (use date.today()) so should keep passing. Frontend testids: daily-details-section, detail-day-{i} (0..N-1), detail-day-date-{i}. For future date-range tests use daterange-trigger + preset-14d + wait ~7s after click. text-transform:uppercase is applied to Ondas/Solunar/Marés labels — Playwright .innerText returns uppercased text so use .toLowerCase() when matching. hero-title testid does NOT exist (use hero-explore-btn or dashboard-section instead).
rca of the issue: No blocker bugs. The single frontend copy issue is minor: DailyDetails OceanBlock says 'Sem dados marítimos (rio / interior)' when marine=null. Root cause: backend daily_details() sets marine=None whenever marine_hourly bucket for that date is empty; this happens both for river points AND for days beyond Open-Meteo marine forecast horizon (~7 days from today). Mitigation option: backend could differentiate the reason (e.g. include marine_status: 'no-marine-point' | 'beyond-horizon') and the frontend would render distinct messages.
